title: Unable to start podman from my mac x86 machine
body:
Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)

/kind bug

**Description**
% podman machine start
INFO[0000] waiting for clients...
INFO[0000] listening tcp://0.0.0.0:7777
INFO[0000] new connection from  to /var/folders/66/znqkqs892nq47lp8mhn3hwfw0000gn/T/podman/qemu_podman-machine-default.sock
Waiting for VM ...
qemu-system-x86_64: -nic user,model=virtio,hostfwd=tcp::55006-:22: Could not set up host forwarding rule 'tcp::55006-:22'
Error: EOF
ERRO[0002] cannot receive packets from , disconnecting: cannot read size from socket: EOF
ERRO[0002] cannot read size from socket: EOF

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. brew install podman
2. podman machine init
3. podman machine start
4. podman info

Describe the results you received:

podman machine start command doesn't work

podman --version
podman version 3.3.1


podman info --debug
Error: failed to create sshClient: Connection to bastion host (ssh://root@linuxhost/run/podman/podman.sock) failed.: dial tcp: lookup linuxhost on 10.5.30.160:53: no such host

comments.body: The last line in your report looks wrong to me.  Did you run a different podman on this machine earlier and used it to connection to other vm's?  Also, the fact that it is trying to connect with root suggests as such.  do you have multiple things in `podman system connection ls` ?

@baude- i ran the podman commands only once on my mac machine. I used podman machine start and it worked first time. The second time I tried, I got back the above error. When I looked up online, I came across this [link](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7301) and followed the comments below.
Contributor
afbjorklund commented on Aug 11, 2020
After installing podman (-remote) on your Mac, you need to set up the environment to point to your Linux server (with podman)

Unfortunately there is no documentation for v2 yet, but something like:
export CONTAINER_HOST=ssh://root@linuxhost/run/podman/podman.sock

Based on that, I ran the export variable and that's why you see multiple things.
Ah that's a super old issue, could you remove the export variable, and try agaib? And if you do a `podman system connection ls` and give us the output, that would also be helpful!

@ashley-cui - I removed the export variable by using "unset CONTAINER_HOST" command and then ran the podman system connection ls command. I get back the same list of connections before running the export command.
Name                         Identity                                     URI
podman-machine-default*      /Users/myuser/.ssh/podman-machine-default  ssh://core@localhost:61443/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
podman-machine-default-root  /Users/myuser/.ssh/podman-machine-default  ssh://root@localhost:61443/run/podman/podman.sock
The "linuxhost" was supposed to be replaced with the actual hostname of your linux server....
@afbjorklund - not sure if i understood what you are saying

If your linux host was called "calvin", then it would be ` ssh://root@calvin/run/podman/podman.sock`

https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-clients-macos-windows
@baude - yes, after a bit of help from tony, i was able to get through this today. thanks for checking
Closing since it looks like this has been resolved
